$comment: Neutral shipped <threads.h> (C11 threads) — FC17.9(a). COMPLETE on elf/pe64 (Cycle 1 non-trampoline slice + Cycle 2 D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-TRAMPOLINES: thrd_create/call_once/thrd_join) and RUNNABLE on macho (D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO closed — the 18 non-trampoline recipes; macho trampolines are a later cycle). THREE-PART design (elf FFI · pe win32-synth · macho pthread-synth): on elf the C11 thread API is a DIRECT libc FFI (glibc>=2.34 exports thrd_*/mtx_*/cnd_*/tss_* from libc.so.6 — verified: readelf shows `mtx_init@@GLIBC_2.34`, a real gcc link of mtx_init WITHOUT -lpthread succeeds; the same libc.so.6 pthread.json already binds). On pe64 the CRT exports NO thrd_*, so each function is a COMPILER-SYNTHESIZED win32 shim over kernel32 (CRITICAL_SECTION / CONDITION_VARIABLE / Fls* / thread APIs — all in windows.json). On macho, libSystem exports NO C11 thrd_*/mtx_*/cnd_*/tss_* (verified — `_thrd_create` undefined at link, no <threads.h> in the SDK) but pthread IS present, so each of the 18 non-trampoline functions is a COMPILER-SYNTHESIZED pthread shim over pthread_mutex_*/pthread_cond_*/pthread_key_*/pthread_self/sched_yield (the Darwin analog of pe's kernel32 shim). ★ The synth VEHICLE + import library are declared on the FORMAT descriptor (`librarySynthesis` in the .format.json), read by synth_threads_shim.cpp — pe→win32/kernel32.dll, macho→pthread/libSystem; the pass dispatches on that declared verb, never on a format identity (this dissolved the pass's former hardcoded kernel32.dll). Helper import names are applyCMangling-mangled per format (macho `_pthread_mutex_init`). ★ DUAL/TRIPLE SAME-NAME ENTRIES per function (the errno __error/__errno_location split precedent): a pe entry (["pe"] + `synthesize`), an elf entry (["elf"], plain FFI, no tag), and — for the 18 non-trampoline recipes — a macho entry (["macho"] + `synthesize`, its own pthread-width signature). The `synthesize` VALUE equals the function NAME (a validated invariant — the loader rejects a mismatch or an unknown recipe id, closed vocabulary). ★ __STDC_NO_THREADS__ is macho-ONLY: threads.h is COMPLETE on elf/pe64 (thrd_create landed → a conforming impl must NOT define the macro there, C11 6.10.8.3), so the c-subset.lang.json predefinedMacros row carries availableObjectFormats:["macho"] — the macro stays only on the macho leg (whose trampolines are deferred). ★ Cycle 2 LANDED thrd_create (pe DIRECT-PASS to CreateThread — the C11 int(*)(void*) start routine has the SAME x64 ABI as Win32 DWORD(*)(void*)), call_once (pe over InitOnceExecuteOnce via the module-scoped __dss_once_tramp adapter), thrd_join (pe multi-block: WaitForSingleObject; if(res) GetExitCodeThread; CloseHandle — StructCfMarkers rederived after finish()). STILL deferred: thrd_equal, mtx_timedlock/cnd_timedwait AND thrd_sleep (D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-TIMED — thrd_sleep's pe timespec read has an unverified time_t/long-width layout, a silent-miscompile risk; thrd_sleep carries only an elf entry); macho trampolines (thrd_create/call_once/thrd_join over pthread) and macho mtx_recursive (D-CSUBSET-C11-THREADS-MACHO-MTX-PLAIN-RECURSIVE). ★ TYPEDEF SIZES (per-format `variants`, mirroring pthread.json): thrd_t elf u64 (pthread_t) / pe ptr<void> (HANDLE) / macho u64. mtx_t elf arr<i64,6>=48 (pthread_mutex_t, arm64 MAX — over-allocates x86-64's 40 harmlessly) / pe arr<i64,5>=40 (CRITICAL_SECTION x64) / macho arr<i64,8>=64 (macOS pthread_mutex_t, measured). cnd_t elf/macho arr<i64,6>=48 (pthread_cond_t) / pe ptr<void>=8 (CONDITION_VARIABLE). once_flag elf i32 (pthread_once_t) / pe ptr<void>=8 (INIT_ONCE) / macho arr<i64,2>=16 (macOS pthread_once_t, measured). tss_t elf/pe u32 (glibc pthread_key_t + Win FLS DWORD) / macho u64 (macOS pthread_key_t=8 — DIVERGES; the macho tss_* signatures use u64). thrd_start_t = ptr<fn(ptr<void>)->i32> (the real C11 int(*)(void*) — Cycle 2, so `thrd_create(&t,worker,&arg)` accepts a bare `worker` cast-free). tss_dtor_t ptr<void>. thrd_t-by-VALUE fns (thrd_current/detach/join) diverge per format so their entries carry DIFFERENT signatures — the dual/triple-entry mechanism handles it. ★ CONSTANTS verified against glibc 2.39 threads.h: thrd_success=0/busy=1/error=2/nomem=3/timedout=4; mtx_plain=0/recursive=1/timed=2 — the SAME values the pe + macho recipes return, so a witness comparing ==thrd_success is portable.
